without the benefit of the NBCUniversal juggernaut.

But Maddow=E2=80=99s deal does not portend business-as-usual across the res=
t of MSNBC. When businesses start to downsize like this, they tend to barbe=
ll: The most essential
talent becomes even more rare and far more essential=E2=80=94and is compens=
ated accordingly=E2=80=94while the rest of the team shrinks and is managed =
for cost. Think of Anna Wintour=E2=80=99s stature at Cond=C3=A9 Nast growin=
g even as its backend units are centralized, the once-robust print titles g=
o digital-only or disappear, top editors make a relative pittance, and Radh=
ika Jones, who never even got a taste of the perks Graydon Carter enjoyed, =
is forced to downsize to a closet-size office=E2=80=A6 and resort to runnin=
g a seemingly unedited account of Cormac McCarthy=E2=80=99s affair with a 1=
6-year-old, but that=E2=80=99s another story.

In MSNBC=E2=80=99s case, much of the backend production will similarly be c=
entralized, while fewer anchors will be required to work longer hours for l=
ess money, all while being judged according to a minute-by-minute cost-bene=
fit analysis of their performance. Meanwhile, the news net is likely to mov=
e out of its historic 30 Rock
headquarters, probably to Stamford, where NBCUniversal houses NBC Sports an=
d its Olympics center, or to the Tech Center in Englewood Cliffs, which is =
already the home of CNBC.

In the early stages of this transition, MSNBC is likely to strike an affili=
ate-style =E2=80=9Ctransaction services agreement=E2=80=9D with NBC News th=
at will allow it to continue using the news network=E2=80=99s resources, as=
 I reported earlier this week. But over the long term, it=E2=80=99s possibl=
e the network will forgo that arrangement and simply rely on the contributi=
ons of journalists from digital news sites who are already willing to come =
on the air for free. If it seems depressing, well, you just wait.

=80=99s once-a-week deal belies her true value to the network: In addition =
to anchoring election coverage and other big news nights, her aura and impr=
imatur elevates the network=E2=80=99s primetime hours even when she=E2=80=
=99s not on the air. =E2=80=9CPeople think she=E2=80=99s on more than she i=
s,=E2=80=9D one MSNBC insider said. Indeed, they think that because she is =
front and center in all of the network=E2=80=99s marketing collateral and c=
reative. By retaining Maddow, the new =E2=80=9CSpinCo=E2=80=9D is fortifyin=
g the revenue stream of the entire bundle, which will now be tasked with st=
riking its own advertising, affiliate, and licensing deals <i>without </i>t=
he benefit of the NBCUniversal juggernaut.<br/><br/>But Maddow=E2=80=99s de=
al does <i>not</i> portend business-as-usual across the rest of MSNBC. When=
 businesses start to downsize like this, they tend to barbell: The most ess=
ential
talent becomes even more <i>rare</i> and far more <i>essential</i>=E2=80=94=
and is compensated accordingly=E2=80=94while the rest of the team shrinks a=
nd is managed for cost. Think of <b>Anna Wintour</b>=E2=80=99s stature at C=
ond=C3=A9 Nast <i>growing</i> even as its backend units are centralized, th=
e once-robust print titles go digital-only or disappear, top editors make a=
 relative pittance, and <b>Radhika Jones</b>, who never even got a taste of=
 the perks <b>Graydon Carter</b> enjoyed, is forced to downsize to a closet=
-size office=E2=80=A6 and resort to running a seemingly unedited account of=
 <b>Cormac McCarthy</b>=E2=80=99s affair with a 16-year-old, but that=E2=80=
=99s another story. <br/><br/>In MSNBC=E2=80=99s case, much of the backend =
production will similarly be centralized, while fewer anchors will be requi=
red to work longer hours for less money, all while being judged according t=
o a minute-by-minute cost-benefit analysis of their performance. Meanwhile,=
 the news net is likely to move out of its historic 30 Rock
headquarters, probably to Stamford, where NBCUniversal houses NBC Sports an=
d its Olympics center, or to the Tech Center in Englewood Cliffs, which is =
already the home of CNBC.<br/><br/>In the early stages of this transition, =
MSNBC is likely to strike an affiliate-style =E2=80=9Ctransaction services =
agreement=E2=80=9D with NBC News that will allow it to continue using the n=
ews network=E2=80=99s resources, as I reported earlier this week. But over =
the long term, it=E2=80=99s possible the network will forgo that arrangemen=
t and simply rely on the contributions of journalists from digital news sit=
es who are already willing to come on the air for free. If it seems depress=
ing, well, you just wait.<br/></td>
         </tr>
